ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation/Potential Breslin Order
Off Calendar Jan-14-2026 Continued To Jun-03-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Lewman for beneficiary Shadia Dabit, who was also present (via Zoom).
Mark Kelly for beneficiary Shafy Dabit, who was also present (via Zoom).
Ted Broomfield for trustee and beneficiary Joseph Dabit (via Zoom).
Renda Dabit, beneficiary in pro per (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) First Account and Report of Trustee; 2) Second Account and Report of Trustee; 3) Petition for Order Suspending and Removing Trustee, et al.; and 4) Status on Mediation / Potential Breslin Order.

Mr. Broomfield reported that he was newly substituted into the case and that discussions among the parties had narrowed the dispute to a limited number of issues suitable for resolution through mediation. Counsel for the parties indicated that they were willing to return to mediation, preferably with the same mediator previously used, in an effort to reach a global resolution.

The Court discussed the possibility of issuing a Breslin order and explained its purpose and effect. After hearing from counsel, the Court determined that issuing a Breslin order would be appropriate and stated it would issue one after the hearing.

The Court continued all matters to June 3, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow the parties to complete mediation and report on the status of settlement.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition On Start Date Of Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Nov-17-2025 Continued To Jan-14-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Monsour for trustee Joseph Dabit, who was also present.
John Lewman for beneficiary Shadia Dabit, who was also present (via Zoom).
Mark Kelly for beneficiary Shafy Dabit, who was also present (via Zoom).
Renda Dabit, beneficiary in pro per.

The matter was called for hearing on 1) First Account and Report of Trustee; 2) Second Account and Report of Trustee; 3) Petition for Orders Suspending and Removing Trustee; 4) Petition for Instructions Regarding Start Date for Accounting.

Mr. Monsour argued that under Babbit v. Superior Court, a trustee's duty to account begins only when the settlor loses the power to revoke the trust. He asserted that the Nabiha Dabit retained revocation power until she conserved on November 18, 2022, and therefore accounting should begin on that date. He also argued that letters from doctors in 2015 and 2021 were hearsay, unauthenticated, and did not evaluate revocation capacity, and that the settlor amended the trust multiple times after 2015, demonstrating capacity.

Mr. Lewman and Mr. Kelly both argued that the settlor lacked the ability to understand financial matters years before the conservatorship, and that the medical letters and declarations showed diminished capacity and susceptibility to undue influence. They requested an accounting back to 2016, when Joseph began acting as trustee, arguing that hudreds of thousands of dollars were reimbursed to the trustee with minimal supporting documentation.

Renda Dabit asked for transparency regarding expenses and questioned the lack of documentation supporting large reimbursements.

After hearing oral argument, the Court held that the presumption of capacity applies, and that the settlor's continued estate-planning activity, amendments, and trustee actions supported the conclusion that she retained revocation power until conserved. Therefore, the start date for the trustee's duty to account is November 18, 2022, the date the settlor was conserved. The Petition for Instructions was taken under submission.

Discussion was held regarding mediation. The Court set a hearing on Status on Mediation/potential Breslin order, and continued all remaining matters to January 14, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Joan Columbini, CSR 5435 (D204)

Petition For An Order: 1. Immediately Suspending & Removing Respondent As Trustee, 2. Appointing A Professional Fiducary As Successor Trustee; 3. Requiring Respondent To Redress The Trust & An Award Of Double Damages; 4. Compelling Respondent To Submit Two Separate Accountings; 5. Enjoining Respondent From Using Trust Funds To Defend Against The Petition; 6. For An Award Of Payment Of Petitioner'S Attorney Fees And Costs

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-25-2025 Continued To Nov-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John Lewman, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Shadia Dabit, Petitioner.
Mark Kelly, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Shafi Dabit, Objector.
David Monsour, Esq., in court, for and with Joseph Dabit, Petitioner.
Renda Dabit, appeared in person, in propria persona, interested party.

Mr. Monsour stated that the objective is to all the properties sold and get to the cash to distribute to all the beneficiaries, that there are still tenants in the rental property, and that he will provide his fees in the accounting.

Mr. Lewman stated that only documents they received were bank statements, that the reimbursements from loans have not been received, that the trustee distributed $260,000 to himself but has not been transparent with the transaction, and he believes the trustee should be suspended. Mr. Kelly agrees that a successor trustee should be appointed.

All matters are continued to November 17, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Reported by, Joan Columbini CSR 5435, 510-367-3043.

MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PES-23-306871 The Estate of Nabiha Dabit and PTR25-308070 In the Matter of Nabiha Dabit 2016 Revocable Trust.

Appearances:
John Lewman, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Shadia Dabit, Petitioner.
Mark Kelly, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Shafi Dabit, Objector.
David Monsour, Esq., in court, for and with Joseph Dabit, Petitioner.
Renda Dabit, appeared in person, in propria persona, interested party.

Councils presented oral arguments to support their position regarding the petitions before the Court. Discussion was held regarding the deeds sent by Ms. Young, that The deeds will need to be recorded, and that it should be done within 30 days.

First account and report of trustee is continued to August 18, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court also orders parties to file the petition by June 16, 2025, that the response be filed by July 21, 2025, and that the reply to be filed by August 7, 2025.

Status of evidentiary hearing, Petition for probate of will and letters for testamentary, and Case management conference are continued to June 9, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court ordered extension of special letters to the next hearing date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.
